type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
The average concentration of arsenic in the waste rock in 2020 is 667 ppm compare to 442 ppm in 2019.  This is mainly due to the change of mineralogy from the new deposit Amaruq being fully mined and processed  in 2020. The total amount of waste rock disposed off on site also went from 12.5MT in 2019 to 30 MT in 2020. This is due to an increase in the production level in 2020 from Whale Tail Pit and the development of the IVR pit.

The average concentration of arsenic in the tailings in 2020 is 1,508 ppm compare to 1,113 ppm in 2019.  This is mainly due to the change of mineralogy from the new deposit Amaruq being fully mined and processed  in 2020. The total amount of tailings disposed off in 2020 is slightly higher than the total volume of 2019

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The average concentration of manganese in the tailings in 2020 is 4,310 ppm compare to 2,345 ppm in 2019.  This is mainly due to the change of mineralogy from the new deposit Amaruq being fully mined and processed  in 2020. The total amount of tailings disposed off in 2020 is slightly higher than the total volume of 2019

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: New estimation method for releases to air

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
The average concentration of nickel in the tailings in 2020 is 691 ppm compare to 294 ppm in 2019.  This is mainly due to the change of mineralogy from the new deposit Amaruq being fully mined and processed  in 2020. The total amount of tailings disposed off in 2020 is slightly higher than the total volume of 2019

Considerably more waste rock was generated in 2020 compared to 2019. The geology in this area is rich in nickel but does not exceed the 1% concentration to include in threshold calculations. However, the nickel release from tailings is large enough to exceed threshold and hence all annual releases need to be included.
